title: Echoes in the Empty Hallway
content:
Every evening after the museum closed, security guards reported hearing footsteps echoing through the central hallway. Cameras never captured anyone, and repeated inspections found nothing unusual.

Architect Laura volunteered to investigate before expensive security upgrades were approved.

She spent an entire night inside the building, carefully observing the sounds.

Near midnight, she noticed something unexpected. The footsteps always followed the arrival of the last subway train beneath the museum.

The building had been constructed above old underground tunnels. Vibrations from passing trains traveled through forgotten structural supports, producing sounds remarkably similar to footsteps inside the long marble corridor.

The explanation disappointed those hoping for something supernatural.

Yet Laura found it strangely beautiful.

The echoes belonged not to invisible visitors but to the city's hidden history, still quietly moving beneath modern streets.

The empty hallway had never been empty at all. It had simply been listening.
